label: Geometry, topology & symmetry

node.label: Geometry, topology & symmetry
node.definition: Distances, manifolds, transformations, invariants, and topology shape spatial and equivariant representations.
node.why_it_matters: In this atlas graph, Geometry, topology & symmetry is modeled as depending on Linear algebra, Calculus & automatic differentiation theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Images, video & spatial grids, Sets, point clouds & meshes, Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings.
examples: atlas downstream link to Images, video & spatial grids
atlas downstream link to Sets, point clouds & meshes
atlas downstream link to Positional, temporal & coordinate encodings
limitations: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Linear algebra, Calculus & automatic differentiation theory.

dependencies.rationale: Geometry, topology & symmetry rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Linear algebra.
Geometry, topology & symmetry rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Calculus & automatic differentiation theory.
